expectations.evidence: Two axes survive (demand, reimbursement) and the reduction is mostly polite rather than silent — hospital wing to watchpoint with a timeline reason, zoning to a deal contingency with a reason. But the lead PT's retention, an unknown resting on her own career choice, is bundled into the demand axis as 'your lead PT's book plus the January surge' with the bundle asserted, never examined; Q4 checks demand against reimbursement but never PT against surge. Her departure then surfaces as a mechanism inside 'demand fades' — the adjective move. The result is the familiar 2×2.
expectations.text: no-forced-square: at least three distinct external unknowns survive to shape the tile space, or any reduction below three arrives as an explicit merge or demotion with the dependency stated — no unknown with a hard consequence silently becomes an adjective on another axis

expectations.evidence: The blip question is folded into the demand axis and branched on; verification arrives only as a Q8 watchpoint ('split your booking data by referral source over the next month or two'). Referral-source history since January already sits in the intake data and is checkable before signing — a knowable treated as fate.
expectations.text: knowable-verified-not-branched: the volume question (blip vs trend) routes to verification from the clinic's own booking and referral data rather than becoming an axis

expectations.evidence: The Stranded tile names the cross-impact on demand: 'a competitor backed by the hospital system, opening into the gap during this stretch, would drain referrals right when the clinic can least absorb the loss.'
expectations.text: correlation-caught: the dependency between the hospital wing and the lead PT (poaching risk) or demand is surfaced — merged, demoted, or named as a cross-impact — rather than the two treated as fully independent

expectations.evidence: No combination is pruned — all four tiles of the 2×2 survive triage with reasons stated, and Q6 confirms each combination claims exactly one tile.
expectations.text: kills-carry-reasons: every pruned combination of landings gets a stated one-line reason; no combination silently disappears between enumeration and telling

expectations.evidence: Grader arithmetic: 2 axes × 2 landings = 4 combinations, 0 kills, 4 tiles carried and 4 told. Consistent, and no budget narration appears. (The narrowness itself is the first assertion's failure, not this one's.)
expectations.text: geometry-consistent: graded by the grader's own arithmetic, never the response's — surviving worlds match the combinations of the named axes minus the stated kills; the response contains no budget narration or tile-count bookkeeping

expectations.evidence: The recommendation itself is committed and carries flips (negotiate a shorter opening term, contingent on the variance, watchpoints decide extension) — but the strategy score table trails it, so the reply ends on a grid rather than the commitment. The same recap-placement failure the snapshot showed in iteration-2's api-pivot run.
expectations.text: recommendation-last-with-flips: ends on a committed sign / don't-sign / negotiate move with named flip conditions; nothing trails the commitment
